stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Investigative journalists are reporters who conduct in-depth research to uncover wrongdoing.
  • Nellie Bly was an investigative journalist who exposed conditions inside the Blackwell’s Island Asylum in 1887.
  • She went undercover by feigning mental illness in order to be committed to the asylum.
  • She lived alongside the patients for ten days, recording firsthand observations of food, staff conduct, and treatment of inmates.
  • Her newspaper series and book Ten Days in a Mad-House (1887) prompted public outcry and a grand jury investigation.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the thoroughness of Nellie Bly’s investigation of Blackwell’s Island Asylum. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Nellie Bly, who investigated Blackwell’s Island Asylum, was an investigative journalist (a reporter who conducts in-depth research to uncover wrongdoing).
As part of her investigation of Blackwell’s Island Asylum, investigative journalist Nellie Bly feigned mental illness.

Nellie Bly not only feigned mental illness to be committed to Blackwell’s Island Asylum but also lived alongside the patients for ten days, recording firsthand observations of food, staff conduct, and treatment of inmates.
Published in 1887, investigative journalist Nellie Bly’s book Ten Days in a Mad-House exposed conditions inside the asylum.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It describes Bly’s investigation and the lengths she went to complete it.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice doesn’t describe how thorough Bly was. Instead, it gives a biographical sketch. Choice B is incorrect. This choice doesn’t describe how thorough Bly was. Bly didn’t only feign illness—she also “lived alongside the patients for ten days, recording firsthand observations.” Choice D is incorrect. This choice describes her book but doesn’t include anything about her investigation.
